# Lesson-media API digest â€” the composer's authoritative import surface

**READING LAW.** This file is the COMPLETE, code-generated inventory of every audio / caption / SFX / timeline symbol the composer imports, and the EXACT path to import each from. Consult it; NEVER `find`/`grep`/`ls`/`cat` the repo, `node_modules`, or `@studio/*` to discover where a symbol lives â€” the answer is here. If a symbol you genuinely need is absent, that is a SKILL/DIGEST GAP: record it as a `pipelineFinding` and proceed; do NOT spelunk.

> âťŚ **NOT exported by `@studio/narration-kit`** (a common wrong import): `LessonAudioLayer`, `LessonBgmLayer`, `LessonSfxLayer`, `LessonCaptionLayer`, `spansToWindows`. These are LOCAL wrappers â€” import them from `../lesson-media/*` (PART A). The `Lesson*Layer` names exist ONLY locally; the kit exports the un-prefixed `AudioLayer`/`CaptionLayer`/`BgmLayer`/`SfxLayer` which you do NOT use directly.

## Copy-paste wiring (the canonical Complete<Lesson> block)

```tsx
import { LessonAudioLayer, LessonBgmLayer, LessonCaptionLayer, LessonSfxLayer } from "../lesson-media/components";
import { spansToWindows } from "../lesson-media/audioMix";
import type { SfxEvent } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer";
import type { CaptionCue } from "../lesson-media/LessonCaptionLayer";
// cue/timeline helpers + types come DIRECTLY from the kit (PART B):
import { reconcileClipTimeline, tokenOnsetFrame } from "@studio/narration-kit";
import type { VoiceClip, PlacedCue } from "@studio/narration-kit";

// â€¦then mount (props are load-bearing â€” LessonCaptionLayer takes `cues`, not `captions`):
//   <LessonAudioLayer voiceClips={<id>VoiceClips} />
//   <LessonCaptionLayer cues={captionCues} />
//   <LessonBgmLayer bed={audioCues.bed} windows={spansToWindows(voiceoverSpans)} totalFrames={<id>Duration} />
//   <LessonSfxLayer events={sfxEvents} />
```

## PART A â€” local wrappers (`src/lesson-media/*`) â€” import from these

### Audio / caption / SFX layers â€” mount in the Complete<Lesson> wrapper  â€” `../lesson-media/components`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `LessonAudioLayer` | component | `import { LessonAudioLayer } from "../lesson-media/components"` | Per-cue voice. Mount ONCE in the Complete wrapper: `<LessonAudioLayer voiceClips={<id>VoiceClips} />`. Never a single teacherAudioSrc/continuous WAV. |
| `LessonBgmLayer` | component | `import { LessonBgmLayer } from "../lesson-media/components"` | Music bed (2nd track). `<LessonBgmLayer bed={audioCues.bed} windows={spansToWindows(voiceoverSpans)} totalFrames={<id>Duration} />` â€” mechanical envelope, you wire it, never tune it. |
| `LessonCaptionLayer` | component | `import { LessonCaptionLayer } from "../lesson-media/components"` | Bottom caption ribbon (kids theme baked in). PROP IS `cues` (NOT `captions`): `<LessonCaptionLayer cues={captionCues} labelWindows={[â€¦]?} />`. |
| `LessonSfxLayer` | component | `import { LessonSfxLayer } from "../lesson-media/components"` | One-shot SFX at composer-owned motion frames. `<LessonSfxLayer events={sfxEvents} />`. Each SfxEvent.fromFrame = cues[id].startFrame + a named layout.ts offset, never a literal. |

### Mix engine (bed duck windows + constants)  â€” `../lesson-media/audioMix`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `BED_UNDUCKED_LINEAR` | const | `import { BED_UNDUCKED_LINEAR } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `DEFAULT_MIX_CONFIG` | const | `import { DEFAULT_MIX_CONFIG } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |

| `DUCK_FACTOR` | const | `import { DUCK_FACTOR } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `DUCK_RELEASE_FRAMES` | const | `import { DUCK_RELEASE_FRAMES } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `EDGE_FADE_FRAMES` | const | `import { EDGE_FADE_FRAMES } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `SFX_DEFAULT_LINEAR` | const | `import { SFX_DEFAULT_LINEAR } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `bedVolume` | helper | `import { bedVolume } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | Deterministic frame-keyed music-bed envelope (used inside LessonBgmLayer; you rarely call it directly). |
| `spansToWindows` | helper | `import { spansToWindows } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | Clip spans â†’ bed-duck windows for <LessonBgmLayer windows={â€¦}>. `spansToWindows(voiceClips.map(c => [c.fromFrame, c.fromFrame + c.durationInFrames]))`. |
| `spokenSpansFromSilences` | helper | `import { spokenSpansFromSilences } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `MixConfig` | type | `import { type MixConfig } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `NarrationWindow` | type | `import { type NarrationWindow } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `SilenceFrames` | type | `import { type SilenceFrames } from "../lesson-media/audioMix"` | _(needs prose)_ |

### SFX vocabulary + registry  â€” `../lesson-media/sfx`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `SFX_REGISTRY` | const | `import { SFX_REGISTRY } from "../lesson-media/sfx"` | The SFX vocabulary map (pop\|chime\|whoosh\|tick\|ta-da â†’ asset). Reach for a registered key, never a raw path. |
| `semitonesToPlaybackRate` | helper | `import { semitonesToPlaybackRate } from "../lesson-media/sfx"` | Pitch-shift an SFX via playbackRate (the rising-pitch-per-count effect). |
| `sfxDefaultVolume` | helper | `import { sfxDefaultVolume } from "../lesson-media/sfx"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `sfxSrc` | helper | `import { sfxSrc } from "../lesson-media/sfx"` | Resolve an SFX key â†’ its asset src. |
| `SfxSound` | type | `import { type SfxSound } from "../lesson-media/sfx"` | The SFX key union: "pop" \| "chime" \| "whoosh" \| "tick" \| "ta-da". |

### SFX event type + concurrency budget  â€” `../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `DEFAULT_SFX_WINDOW_FRAMES` | const | `import { DEFAULT_SFX_WINDOW_FRAMES } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `MAX_CONCURRENT_AUDIO` | const | `import { MAX_CONCURRENT_AUDIO } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | Ceiling on simultaneously-audible SFX one-shots. |
| `assertConcurrentAudioBudget` | helper | `import { assertConcurrentAudioBudget } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | Throws AT MODULE LOAD if the SFX schedule exceeds MAX_CONCURRENT_AUDIO. Call once after building sfxEvents. |
| `sampleAudioEvents` | helper | `import { sampleAudioEvents } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | Deterministic down-sample a dense SFX schedule to â‰¤ max (even stride, never Math.random). |
| `BudgetedSfxEvent` | type | `import { type BudgetedSfxEvent } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `SfxEvent` | type | `import { type SfxEvent } from "../lesson-media/LessonSfxLayer"` | One scheduled SFX: { sound, fromFrame, volume?, semitones? }. Build an SfxEvent[] then pass to <LessonSfxLayer events={â€¦} />. |

### Caption cue type + label-window helpers  â€” `../lesson-media/LessonCaptionLayer`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `filterCaptionsAroundLabels` | helper | `import { filterCaptionsAroundLabels } from "../lesson-media/LessonCaptionLayer"` | Drop caption cues whose midpoint falls inside an on-screen label window (one representation per beat). |
| `CaptionCue` | type | `import { type CaptionCue } from "../lesson-media/LessonCaptionLayer"` | One caption row: { fromFrame, toFrame, text\|tokens }. Build the array yourself when a cue has a gap (clamp toFrame to narrationEndFrame). |
| `LabelWindow` | type | `import { type LabelWindow } from "../lesson-media/LessonCaptionLayer"` | { startFrame, endFrame } â€” a window during which an in-canvas label suppresses the ribbon. |

### Caption keyword rewrite (redundancy gate)  â€” `../lesson-media/captionKeywords`

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `withCaptionKeywords` | helper | `import { withCaptionKeywords } from "../lesson-media/captionKeywords"` | Rewrite each cue's caption to a short authored keyword anchor (redundancy gate). Apply to the reconciled cue list. |
| `CaptionKeywordMap` | type | `import { type CaptionKeywordMap } from "../lesson-media/captionKeywords"` | _(needs prose)_ |

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `CAPTION_BAND` | const | `import { CAPTION_BAND } from "../lesson-media/captionBand"` | The one shared caption-ribbon footprint [x,y,w,h] the measured gate treats as no-go for teaching marks. |

| symbol | kind | import | use |

## PART B â€” cue / timeline helpers + types â€” import directly from `@studio/narration-kit`

_These are NOT re-exported by the local wrappers; import them straight from the kit. (The un-prefixed layer components here â€” AudioLayer/CaptionLayer â€” are KIT-INTERNAL; use the PART A `Lesson*Layer` wrappers instead.)_

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `AlignedLessonCue` | type | `import { type AlignedLessonCue } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | An ASR-aligned cue (phrase + token onsets) â€” the pre-reconcile shape. |
| `AudioLayer` | component | `import { AudioLayer } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | KIT-INTERNAL â€” wrapped locally as LessonAudioLayer. Use the wrapper, not this. |
| `CaptionCue` | type | `import { type CaptionCue } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | One caption row: { fromFrame, toFrame, text\|tokens }. Build the array yourself when a cue has a gap (clamp toFrame to narrationEndFrame). |
| `CaptionLayer` | component | `import { CaptionLayer } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | KIT-INTERNAL â€” wrapped locally as LessonCaptionLayer. Use the wrapper, not this. |
| `CaptionLayerTheme` | type | `import { type CaptionLayerTheme } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `ClipCue` | type | `import { type ClipCue } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `cueMap` | helper | `import { cueMap } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | Index the reconciled cues by id for O(1) lookup. |
| `cueToCaption` | helper | `import { cueToCaption } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | Derive a CaptionCue from a reconciled cue (mechanical; Wave 3.5). |
| `findBoundarySilence` | helper | `import { findBoundarySilence } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `PlacedCue` | type | `import { type PlacedCue } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | A reconciled cue: carries startFrame/endFrame/narrationStartFrame/narrationEndFrame/gapFrames/holdFrames/tokenOnsets. |
| `reconcileClipTimeline` | helper | `import { reconcileClipTimeline } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | Wave-3.5 reconcile: chains per-cue clips â†’ { cues, voiceClips, durationFrames }. Used in the <id>LessonTimeline.ts module. |
| `reconcileCueTimeline` | helper | `import { reconcileCueTimeline } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | Cue-level reconcile variant (pre-clip lessons). |
| `SilenceInterval` | type | `import { type SilenceInterval } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `stepFramesFromOnsets` | helper | `import { stepFramesFromOnsets } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | Absolute step frames from a cue's token onsets (count-up / read-along enumerations). |
| `tokenOnsetFrame` | helper | `import { tokenOnsetFrame } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | ABSOLUTE frame of a cue's i-th spoken token (already start-offset). Use for per-token/per-count binding â€” never a fixed grid. |
| `VisualBudgetMap` | type | `import { type VisualBudgetMap } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | _(needs prose)_ |
| `VoiceClip` | type | `import { type VoiceClip } from "@studio/narration-kit"` | One per-cue voice clip: { src, fromFrame, durationInFrames }. Pass the array straight to <LessonAudioLayer voiceClips={â€¦} />. |

## PART C â€” underlying `@studio/sound-kit` surface (reference)

_You normally reach these through the PART A wrappers (`../lesson-media/sfx`, `audioMix`, `LessonBgmLayer`, `LessonSfxLayer`). Listed for completeness._

| symbol | kind | import | use |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `SfxEvent` | type | `import { type SfxEvent } from "@studio/sound-kit"` | One scheduled SFX: { sound, fromFrame, volume?, semitones? }. Build an SfxEvent[] then pass to <LessonSfxLayer events={â€¦} />. |
| `SfxLayer` | component | `import { SfxLayer } from "@studio/sound-kit"` | KIT-INTERNAL â€” wrapped locally as LessonSfxLayer. Use the wrapper, not this. |
